article_meta.title: EntrepreneursMeet Angelica Nwandu: Founder Of The Shade Room, Instagram's 3rd Most Engaged ProfileByTommy

reasoning: The model's response has some issues but isn't severely flawed. It correctly avoids flagging quoted text as author bias (good attribution rule compliance). However, the two items it does flag are questionable. The 'Instagram's 3rd Most Engaged Profile' claim could defensibly be flagged as unsubstantiated since no source is cited, though this is a somewhat weak finding. The second item about 'shared her vision with friends' is a stretch - this is more of a general journalistic critique than a specific bias instance. More importantly, the article is essentially a profile/feature piece that reads like promotional content, and the model misses the overall slant and laudatory framing throughout (e.g., 'impressive 17.7m followers', the entirely one-sided positive narrative structure). The model also outputs markdown fences despite being told not to.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Neither flagged item is from quoted text. Both are from the author's own prose/framing. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The 'Instagram's 3rd Most Engaged Profile' is a defensible flag as unsubstantiated - the ranking claim has no cited source. The second flag about 'shared her vision with friends' being an omission of source attribution is a stretch; paraphrasing a subject's account in a profile piece is standard journalism, not really a bias instance. So roughly 1 of 2 items is questionable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model misses the overall slant of the piece - it reads as a promotional profile with no critical perspective. 'impressive 17.7m followers' uses a subjective qualifying adjective in the author's voice. The piece is entirely one-sided, presenting only Angelica's perspective with no independent assessment of her claims or business. These are defensible bias instances (Slant, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives) that were missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The 'Unsubstantiated Claims' label for the Instagram ranking is reasonable. The 'Omission of Source Attribution' label for the second item is a stretch - this doesn't quite fit the standard definition. A better criticism would be the overall Slant of the article. So one label is defensible, one is somewhat misapplied.
